content.problemStatement:
One day, Saeed was teaching a string compression algorithm. This algorithm finds all maximal substrings which contains only one character repeated one or more times (a substring is maximal if it we cannot add one character to its left or right without breaking this property) and replaces each such substring by the string "cK", where KKK is the length of the substring and ccc is the only character it contains. For example, "aabaaa" is compressed to "a2b1a3".

Saeed wanted to check if the students understood the algorithm, so he wrote a string SSS on the board and asked the students if the algorithm is effective on SSS, i.e. if the string created by compressing SSS is strictly shorter than SSS. Help them answer this question.
constraints: SSS may consist of only lowercase English letters.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if the algorithm is effective on SSS or `"NO"` if it is not.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

The compressed string of "bbbbbbbbbbaa" is "b10a2", which is shorter.
The compressed string of "c" is "c1", which is not shorter than "c".
The compressed string of "aaaaaaaaaabcdefgh" is "a10b1c1d1e1f1g1h1", which is not shorter than "aaaaaaaaaabcdefgh" (both strings have length 171717).
